careSummary: Let the top 2-3 cm of soil dry between waterings. Loves bright, indirect light and humidity. Wipe leaves to keep them glossy and provide a moss pole for the aerial roots to climb.
Extremely drought tolerant. Water only when the soil is fully dry, roughly every two weeks. Tolerates low light but grows faster in brighter spots. Avoid overwatering to prevent root rot.
A forgiving trailing vine. Water when the top few centimetres of soil dry out. Thrives in medium light and tolerates low light. Prune regularly to encourage bushy, full growth.
Needs consistent bright, indirect light and dislikes being moved. Water when the top few centimetres dry out. Sensitive to overwatering and drafts. Dust the large leaves to maximise light absorption.
Stores water in rhizomes, making it very drought tolerant. Water sparingly once the soil is dry. Handles low light well and rarely needs pruning. A near-indestructible beginner plant.
Enjoys consistently moist soil and droops dramatically when thirsty, then recovers after watering. Prefers medium, indirect light. Remove spent flowers and yellow leaves to keep it tidy.
Fast-growing and easy. Water when the top of the soil dries. Produces baby plantlets that can be propagated. Prefers bright, indirect light; sensitive to fluoride in tap water.
Glossy-leaved tree that likes bright, indirect light. Let the top soil dry between waterings. Wipe leaves to keep them shiny. Prune to control height and encourage branching.
A succulent that needs bright light and infrequent, deep watering once the soil is completely dry. Use well-draining cactus soil. Harvest outer leaves for their soothing gel.
Loves high humidity and consistently moist soil. Keep away from dry heat and direct sun. Mist regularly and trim brown fronds. Place in a humid bathroom for best results.
A trailing vine that likes evenly moist soil and medium light. Prune frequently to keep it full and manageable. Prefers cooler temperatures and good airflow to avoid pests.
A long-lived succulent needing bright, direct light and infrequent watering once the soil dries fully. Use gritty, well-draining soil. Prune to shape into a tree-like form.
An easy trailing plant with heart-shaped leaves. Water when the top soil dries. Tolerates a range of light from low to bright indirect. Pinch back stems to keep it bushy.
Round, coin-shaped leaves on upright stems. Water when the top soil dries and rotate regularly for even growth. Prefers bright, indirect light. Produces offshoots that are easy to propagate.
Prized for its striped silver foliage. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. Use filtered or distilled water to avoid leaf browning. Avoid direct sun, which fades the patterns.
A trailing succulent with bead-like leaves. Water sparingly once the soil dries; the pearls plump up when hydrated. Needs bright, indirect light. Avoid sitting water to prevent rot.
A feathery palm that likes bright, indirect light and slightly moist soil. Sensitive to fluoride; use filtered water. Trim only fully brown fronds. Boosts indoor humidity nicely.
A compact, low-light tolerant palm. Water when the top soil dries and avoid overwatering. Rarely needs pruning beyond removing dead fronds. A classic, easy-care indoor palm.
A spiky-leaved tree that tolerates neglect. Let soil dry well between waterings and use filtered water to avoid leaf tip browning. Prune the cane to control height and shape.
Famous for vivid multicoloured leaves that need bright light to stay vibrant. Keep soil evenly moist and humidity high. Dislikes being moved and may drop leaves when stressed.
Known for glossy red spathes. Water when the top soil dries and keep humidity high. Prefers bright, indirect light. Remove spent blooms and yellow leaves to encourage flowering.
A dramatic, large-leaved plant that needs lots of bright, direct light to bloom. Water when the top soil dries. Wipe leaves and remove damaged foliage. Needs space to spread.
A trailing or climbing vine with delicately holed leaves. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. Provide a trellis or let it trail. Prune to encourage fuller growth.
A heavily variegated pothos. Water when the top soil dries. Needs brighter light than plain pothos to maintain the white marbling. Prune trailing vines to keep it dense.
A delicate trailing plant with heart-shaped leaves on thin vines. Water sparingly once the soil dries. Loves bright, indirect light. Easy to propagate from cuttings or tubers.
A rosette-forming succulent needing bright, direct light and infrequent deep watering once soil is dry. Use gritty soil. Remove dead lower leaves. Avoid water sitting in the rosette.
A small succulent with white-striped leaves. Water when soil is fully dry, about every two weeks. Tolerates lower light than most succulents. Rarely needs pruning beyond removing dead leaves.
A forest cactus that blooms in winter. Keep soil lightly moist when growing and drier in autumn to trigger flowers. Prefers bright, indirect light. Pinch segments to encourage branching.
A flowering succulent with clusters of bright blooms. Water once the soil dries and give it plenty of bright light. Deadhead spent flowers to prolong blooming. Avoid soggy soil.
A compact flowering houseplant. Water from below to keep leaves dry and avoid spotting. Prefers medium, indirect light and stable warmth. Remove spent blooms to encourage more flowers.
Grown for striking patterned leaves. Keep soil lightly moist but never soggy and humidity high. Prefers medium, indirect light. Avoid wetting the leaves to prevent powdery mildew.
A leafy plant with cream-marked foliage. Water when the top soil dries and keep in medium, indirect light. Sap is irritating, so wear gloves when pruning. Wipe leaves to keep them clean.
An easy, colourful foliage plant that tolerates low light. Water when the top soil dries. Darker varieties handle shade; variegated ones prefer more light. Minimal pruning required.
Glossy umbrella-shaped leaflets on upright stems. Water when the top soil dries and provide bright, indirect light. Prune to keep it bushy and prevent legginess. Avoid overwatering.
A drought-tolerant plant with sword-like leaves. Needs bright light and infrequent watering once the soil dries. Use well-draining soil. Remove dead lower leaves as it grows.
Not a true palm; it stores water in its swollen base, making it very drought tolerant. Water sparingly once soil dries. Loves bright light. Trim brown leaf tips as needed.
A soft-needled indoor conifer. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. Prefers bright, indirect light. Do not cut the top, as it will not regrow a central leader. Rotate for even growth.
Nearly indestructible and tolerant of low light and neglect. Water when the top soil dries. Slow-growing, so pruning is rarely needed. Wipe leaves occasionally to remove dust.
Named for leaves that fold up at night. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high; use filtered water. Prefers medium, indirect light. Trim leggy stems to promote bushy growth.
A compact plant with veined leaves that wilts quickly when dry but recovers fast. Keep soil consistently moist and humidity high. Prefers medium light. Pinch tips to keep it compact.
Has striped, watermelon-patterned leaves. Water when the top soil dries; the thick leaves store water. Prefers medium, indirect light. Compact growth means little pruning is needed.
A trailing plant with waxy leaves and fragrant star-shaped flowers. Water once the soil dries. Needs bright, indirect light to bloom. Do not remove the flower spurs, as they reflower.
A fast-growing trailing plant with purple-striped leaves. Water when the top soil dries. Needs bright, indirect light to keep its colour. Pinch frequently to prevent leggy growth.
Grown for its spotted pink or white foliage. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity moderate. Prefers bright, indirect to medium light. Pinch back regularly to keep it compact and colourful.
Often grown in water; change the water weekly and keep roots submerged. In soil, keep lightly moist. Use filtered water to avoid tip burn. Prefers medium, indirect light.
Recognised by its braided trunk. Water when the top few centimetres dry out; avoid soggy soil. Prefers bright, indirect light and moderate humidity. Prune to maintain its shape.
A rare, delicate vine with leaves that are mostly holes. Requires very high humidity and consistently moist soil. Prefers bright, indirect light. Handle gently as the foliage is fragile.
Dramatic arrow-shaped leaves with bold veins. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity very high. Prefers bright, indirect light. Goes dormant in winter; reduce watering then.
A self-heading philodendron with white pinstriped leaves. Water when the top soil dries. Needs bright, indirect light to maintain its variegation. Wipe leaves and remove any reverted growth.
A graceful tree that drops leaves when stressed or moved. Water when the top soil dries and keep in stable bright, indirect light. Prune to shape and avoid sudden environmental changes.
A larger umbrella tree with big glossy leaflets. Water when the top soil dries and provide bright, indirect light. Prune to control its rapid height and encourage a fuller canopy.
Not a true fern; it has feathery foliage and tiny thorns. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. Prefers bright, indirect to medium light. Trim yellow stems to keep it tidy.
An epiphytic fern often mounted on boards. Soak or mist when the base dries out and keep humidity high. Prefers bright, indirect light. Do not remove the brown shield fronds at the base.
An elegant, slow-growing palm that tolerates lower light. Water when the top soil dries; avoid overwatering. Trim only dead fronds. Wipe fronds to remove dust and deter pests.
Glossy-leaved plant that can eventually produce coffee cherries. Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. Prefers bright, indirect light. Prune to keep it bushy and manageable indoors.
